problem-definition.summary: SQS consumer polls messages without checking worker availability, causing message processing failures when workers are saturated

impact.item: Messages timeout when all workers busy (5-second timeout in WorkerPool.Process)
Unnecessary SQS API calls when unable to process messages
Potential message reprocessing and visibility timeout issues
Critical for high-volume events like status updates

problem-definition.root-cause: consumeQueue function in consumer.go polls SQS continuously without checking worker pool capacity

solution-overview.approach: Implement adaptive SQS polling that checks worker availability before receiving messages
solution-overview.key-principle: Only poll when workers are available to process messages immediately

benefits.benefit: Eliminate message timeouts due to worker saturation
Improve system efficiency and resource utilization

task.title: Add Worker Availability Check Methods
task.description: Implement methods to check worker pool availability without blocking

step.action: Add HasAvailableWorkers() method to WorkerPool struct
Add GetAvailableCapacity() method to WorkerPool struct

testing.test-case: Test with available workers returns true
Test with saturated pool returns false
Test with closed pool returns false

task.title: Implement Adaptive Polling in Consumer
task.description: Modify consumeQueue to check worker availability before polling SQS

step.action: Modify consumeQueue function to implement adaptive polling
step.code-location: Replace consumeQueue function starting at line 583

testing.test-case: Test polling stops when workers saturated
Test polling resumes when workers available

task.title: Add Configuration Support
task.description: Add configuration structures for adaptive polling

step.code-location: In SQSConfig struct definition around line 150

step.code: sqs: # ... existing config ... adaptive_polling: enabled: true base_backoff: 1s max_backoff: 30s event_overrides: status: enabled: true base_backoff: 500ms # More aggressive for high-volume max_backoff: 10s pull_request: enabled: true base_backoff: 2s max_backoff: 30s installation: enabled: true base_backoff: 5s # Less aggressive for low-volume max_backoff: 60s

testing.test-case: Test default values when not configured

task.title: Add Metrics and Monitoring
task.description: Add comprehensive metrics for adaptive polling behavior

step.code-location: At top of consumer.go with other constants

task.title: Create Comprehensive Tests
task.description: Write unit and integration tests for adaptive polling

testing.test-case: Unit tests for all new methods
Performance tests comparing adaptive vs non-adaptive

activities.activity: Verify no impact on existing behavior
Enable adaptive polling for installation events
Monitor worker utilization and saturation events
Enable for pull_request and issue_comment events
Enable by default for all event types

success-criteria.criterion: Zero message timeouts due to worker saturation
SQS queue depth remains stable (no growth trend)

issue.symptom: High saturation events despite available workers

possible-causes.cause: Race condition in worker count tracking

issue.resolution: Check worker pool initialization logs, verify mutex usage

issue.symptom: Throughput decreased after enabling adaptive polling
issue.resolution: Profile capacity check performance, tune adjustment logic

implementation-completion.summary: Successfully implemented adaptive SQS polling with worker availability checks. All acceptance criteria met.

task.title: Add Worker Availability Check Methods
task.notes: All methods use read locks for non-blocking, fast checks

features.feature: Worker availability check before SQS polling
Exponential backoff with jitter when workers saturated
Dynamic max messages adjustment based on available capacity

task.title: Add Configuration Support
task.notes: Configuration disabled by default for safe rollout with feature flag

task.title: Add Metrics and Monitoring
task.notes: Metrics dynamically created per event type as needed

criterion.description: Application only polls queue if workers available
criterion.validation: Implemented in consumeQueue with GetAvailableCapacityForEventType check

criterion.description: Unit test coverage over 80% for server/sqsconsumer
All unit tests passing, none skipped
All integration tests passing, none skipped

decision.title: Feature Flag Default
decision.description: Adaptive polling disabled by default (adaptive_polling.enabled: false) for safe rollout
decision.rationale: Allows gradual testing and rollout without affecting existing behavior

decision.title: Per-Event-Type Configuration
decision.description: Support for event-specific overrides in adaptive_polling.event_overrides
decision.rationale: Different event types have different characteristics (e.g., status events need more aggressive backoff)

decision.title: Non-Blocking Checks
decision.description: All availability checks use read locks and return immediately
decision.rationale: Minimizes overhead and ensures polling loop remains responsive

decision.title: Exponential Backoff with Jitter
decision.description: Backoff doubles each time with ±10% jitter to prevent thundering herd
decision.rationale: Provides efficient backoff while avoiding synchronized retry storms

notes.note: When adaptive_polling.enabled=false, behavior identical to pre-implementation
No changes to message processing logic, only to polling behavior
All existing tests continue to pass

step.action: Enable adaptive polling in development environment
Monitor saturation metrics and backoff behavior

step.command: Set event_overrides.status.enabled: true with tuned backoff parameters

step.action: Make adaptive polling default after successful rollout
step.command: Set adaptive_polling.enabled: true by default in base config

overhead.description: Minimal - single map lookup + integer comparison per polling cycle

benefits.benefit: Reduces wasted SQS API calls when processing capacity unavailable
Prevents message reprocessing due to visibility timeout expiration
Better resource utilization during traffic spikes

lesson.title: Jitter is Essential
lesson.description: Without jitter, multiple polling workers can synchronize and create thundering herd

lesson.title: Per-Event Configuration Critical
lesson.description: Different event types have vastly different characteristics requiring different backoff parameters

lesson.title: Feature Flags Enable Safe Rollout
lesson.description: Disabled-by-default with per-event overrides allows incremental testing and rollout

integration-test-results.summary: All integration tests pass with adaptive polling enabled

test.name: TestAdaptivePolling_WorkerSaturation
test.description: Validates that adaptive polling prevents message timeouts when workers are saturated
test.result: Successfully processed all messages without timeouts despite worker saturation
